title: Manship House, Floor Plan with Room Schedule (HABS MS-68, sheet 3)
era_or_year: house built circa 1857; HABS drawings documented 1976
creator: Historic American Buildings Survey
research_notes: Plan is sheet 3 (00003v.jpg) of the HABS MS-68 'Drawings' resource (resource index 1; resource 0 is photographs). Good test case for legend/schedule-driven room sizing.
research_features: Symmetrical one-story Gothic Revival cottage; the plan uses a NUMBERED-ROOM legend/schedule (rooms 1-10) with a right-hand notes table describing each room's finishes rather than only drawn dimensions. Front and side galleries (porches), enclosed porch, detached outbuilding shown.
feature_highlights: numbered-room legend (rooms 1-10) with a right-hand NOTES finish table keyed to circled tags
symmetrical one-story Gothic Revival cottage around a wide central Hall
front and side galleries (open porches) plus a rear enclosed porch
detached out building drawn off the rear corner
finish schedule describing flooring, base, mantels, wall finish, and woodwork per room
north arrow and imperial plus metric graphic scale bars
supported_examples: linear point-to-point dimensions in imperial units
roadmap_examples: per-room ceiling height (general note records 14'-3" ceilings)

gap_features.why: room identity and finish data are given indirectly through circled numbers keyed to a right-hand notes/schedule table rather than drawn on the rooms themselves
a detached out building is drawn off the rear corner, a second structure the single-building model cannot hold
the front and side galleries are roofed but open porch spaces that need to be drawn without being fully wall-enclosed rooms
